pick.rationale: Utah State enters this 8/9 matchup as the stronger team by NET ranking (#26 vs. Villanova's implied #33-36), having swept both the Mountain West regular season and conference tournament titles with a 28-6 record and a 4-4 Quad 1 mark that reflects genuine résumé quality. Villanova's case is meaningfully undermined by the season-ending ACL tear suffered by starting forward Matt Hodge in late February, depleting their frontcourt depth heading into single-elimination play. The betting market and expert analysts flagged Utah State as underseeded at 9, with their high-octane offense led by seniors MJ Collins Jr. and Mason Falsev presenting a difficult matchup for a Villanova team now operating shorthanded up front. While Villanova's Big East battle-testing and KenPom #33 efficiency rating keep this genuinely competitive, Utah State's superior NET, hot finish, and Villanova's injury context tip this classic coin-flip game to the Aggies.

reasoningStep.title: Ratings, Injury, and Matchup Context: Villanova vs. Utah State
reasoningStep.summary: Utah State holds a superior NET ranking (#26) despite being seeded 9th, having won both the Mountain West regular season and tournament titles at 28-6. Villanova (KenPom #33, Torvik #36, AdjEM +19.94, 24-8 Big East) is the higher seed but suffered a critical frontcourt injury when starting forward Matt Hodge tore his ACL in late February. The betting market and analysts flagged Utah State as underseeded, and their offensive firepower creates a credible path against a depleted Villanova roster.
evidence: Villanova KenPom #33, Torvik #36, AdjEM +19.94, 24-8 Big East record; defense ranked 37th nationally (AdjDE 100.4)
Utah State NET #26, 28-6 overall, 15-5 Mountain West, 4-4 Quad 1, 9-1 Quad 2 — won both MWC regular season and tournament titles
Matt Hodge (Villanova starting forward, 9.2 PPG/3.6 RPG) suffered season-ending ACL tear on Feb 28, 2026 — confirmed by ESPN and AP News
BettingPros noted Utah State is rated higher in the NET despite being the 9-seed; ESPN bracket reaction called Utah State underseeded
Utah State won Mountain West Tournament (3rd title), defeating New Mexico 94-90 in the final — entered tournament on a high note
Search result headline confirmed Utah State's high-octane offense led by MJ Collins Jr. and Mason Falsev as decisive factors

result.text: Villanova vs. Utah State: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Villanova vs. Utah State: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 16, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 16, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for Villanova vs. Utah State. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: Villanova vs. Utah State Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: Villanova vs. Utah State Villanova 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview First-year Villanova head coach Kevin Willard's team finished 24-8 with a 15-5 Big East record, ranking inside the top 35 in KenPom and the NET. The program is back in the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 2022, which is kind of insane for such a high-profile program. Villanova's defining player is freshman Acaden Lewis, who ended the regular season at 12.3 points, 5.3 assists, 3.1 rebounds, and 1.9 steals per game in 30.5 minutes while carrying the highest usage rate on the team. He is one of the better freshman guards in the country and was the primary reason Villanova climbed nearly 20 spots from its preseason KenPom projection. Tyler Perkins leads the team in scoring at 13.7 points per game while shooting 37% from three, providing the perimeter shooting along with Devin Askew, a 41% marksman. Duke Brennan is 11th nationally in rebounding at just over 10 boards per game, giving the Wildcats a physical interior presence. Depth is an issue after Matt Hodge's season-ending ACL tear and Zion Stanford's early departure left Villanova with essentially two rotation players over 6-6. In the Big East Tournament loss to Georgetown, the Hoyas outrebounded them 46-25 in a 14-point defeat. Lewis went 2-for-9. Devin Askew went 1-for-9 from three. When the supporting cast checks out against physical interior opponents or teams with size advantages, the offense stalls and the rebounding becomes a structural problem. Lewis is the reason to believe Villanova can get a first-round win. The depth is the reason to question anything beyond it. Utah State 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Jerrod Calhoun's Aggies went

result.text: Mountain West - 2026 NET Rankings and Quad Wins Mountain West - 2026 NET Rankings and Quad Wins NET Ranks through Mar 15 2026 and Game Results through Mar 15 2026 Mountain West Rank --> Prev --> School --> Record --> Conf Record --> Q1 --> Q2 --> Q3 --> Q4 --> Rank Prev School Record Conf Record Q1 Q2 Q3 Q4 26 26 Utah St. 28-6 15-5 4-4 9-1 9-1 5-0 46 46 New Mexico 23-10 13-7 2-7 6-1 4-2 10-0 47 47 San Diego St. 22-11 14-6 3-8 6-2 6-1 6-0 60 60 Boise St. 20-12 12-8 2-6 6-3 8-1 3-1 70 70 Nevada 22-12 12-8 1-6 5-4 11-0 5-2 71 71 Grand Canyon 20-12 13-7 3-5 3-4 4-2 10-1 87 87 Colorado St. 21-12 11-9 3-5 3-5 5-1 9-1 99 99 Wyoming 18-14 9-11 1-7 1-5 3-2 12-0 111 111 UNLV 17-16 11-9 4-5 5-4 2-4 5-3 153 153 Fresno St. 13-19 7-13 0-7 0-8 5-2 7-2 244 244 San Jose St. 9-24 3-17 0-7 2-10 0-7 6-0 350 350 Air Force 3-29 0-20 0-7 0-9 0-8 3-5 Back to Team List This tool will display the breakdown of quad wins/losses for NCAA DI Men's Basketball Teams. This is helpful to determine the chances of teams being selected for the NCAA Tournament during March Madness. Some example use cases would be: to visualize how close a Q2 opponent is to becoming a higher quality Q1 opponent; to determine whether one team has more quality wins than another team; to check how many opportunities for quality wins remain on a team's schedule. This app is not associated with the NCAA or any institutions represented on this website. All marks and logos are trademarks of their respective owners. A green up arrow means that the team's NET rank is trending upwards compared to the previous rank, while a red down arrow means that the teams's NET rank is trending downwards. A gray neutral icon means that the ranking has not changed. The previous rank can be seen by opening the team's page. An opponent's current NET ranking is displayed in parenthesis next to the trending icon The quadrant system is used to identify which teams have more quality wins when it comes to Selection Sunday for the NCAA Tournament. Beating a Top-30 Team at home is considered equally as difficult as beating a Top-75 Team away: Quadrant 1: Home 1-30, Neutral 1-50, Away 1-75. Quadrant 2: Home 31-75, Neutral 51-100, Away 76-135. Quadrant 3: Home 76-160, Neutral 101-200, Away 135-240. Quadrant 4: Home 161-353, Neutral 201-353, Away 241-353. result.text: Utah State receives 9 seed, will face Villanova in NCAA Tournament – Deseret News Deseret News Deseret Magazine Church News Deseret News Deseret Magazine Print Subscriptions Newsletter Sign Up Latest Latest Politics Politics Utah Utah Sports Sports Opinion Opinion Magazine Magazine More More Sign In Register Sign In Search Go Utah State Aggies Sports Utah State receives 9 seed, will play Villanova in NCAA Tournament’s first round Published: March 15, 2026, 4:21 p.m. MDT View Comments Share The Utah State Aggies pose for a photo with some fans and the Mountain West championship trophy following their 94-90 victory over New Mexico March 7 at the Spectrum in Logan. (Jeff Hunter) Jeff Hunter By Jackson Payne Jackson has been writing about sports for the Deseret News since 2023. Your browser does not support the audio element. Play audio NEW: Try Article Audio NEW: Try Article Audio Audio quality: | Skip back 15 seconds Play audio Skip forward 15 seconds 00:00 00:00 Decrease playback rate 1.0 x Increase playback rate 00:00 / 00:00 Skip back 15 seconds Play audio Skip forward 15 seconds - 0.5x 0.6x 0.7x 0.8x 0.9x 1.0x 1.1x 1.2x 1.3x 1.4x 1.5x 1.6x 1.7x 1.8x 1.9x 2.0x 2.1x 2.2x 2.3x 2.4x 2.5x 2.6x 2.7x 2.8x 2.9x 3.0x + After capturing the Mountain West regular season and tournament titles, Utah State is ready for another trip to the NCAA Tournament. The Aggies have earned a 9 seed in the West Region. They will face Villanova in the Round of 64 on Friday in San Diego, as announced during Sunday’s bracket reveal show on CBS. This will be Utah State’s fourth consecutive NCAA Tournament appearance and 27th ticket to the Big Dance in program history. In Jerrod Calhoun’s first season with the program last year, the Aggies fell 72-47 to UCLA in a 7-10 round of 64 matchup. Since the school’s last Sweet 16 appearance in 1970, Utah State has a 2-20 record in NCAA Tournament contests, with the most recent such win coming in 2024 against 9-seeded TCU. Villanova ranks No. 33 in KenPom, having gone 24-8 in Kevin Willard’s first year piloting the Wildcats to finish third in the Big East. Should the Aggies take care of business Friday against Villanova, they would face the winner of a 1-16 matchup between Arizona and LIU on Sunday. Join the Conversation Looking for comments? modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: Villanova vs. Utah State Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: Villanova vs. Utah State Villanova 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview First-year Villanova head coach Kevin Willard's team finished 24-8 with a 15-5 Big East record, ranking inside the top 35 in KenPom and the NET. The program is back in the NCAA Tournament for the first time since 2022, which is kind of insane for such a high-profile program. Villanova's defining player is freshman Acaden Lewis, who ended the regular season at 12.3 points, 5.3 assists, 3.1 rebounds, and 1.9 steals per game in 30.5 minutes while carrying the highest usage rate on the team. He is one of the better freshman guards in the country and was the primary reason Villanova climbed nearly 20 spots from its preseason KenPom projection. Tyler Perkins leads the team in scoring at 13.7 points per game while shooting 37% from three, providing the perimeter shooting along with Devin Askew, a 41% marksman. Duke Brennan is 11th nationally in rebounding at just over 10 boards per game, giving the Wildcats a physical interior presence. Depth is an issue after Matt Hodge's season-ending ACL tear and Zion Stanford's early departure left Villanova with essentially two rotation players over 6-6. In the Big East Tournament loss to Georgetown, the Hoyas outrebounded them 46-25 in a 14-point defeat. Lewis went 2-for-9. Devin Askew went 1-for-9 from three. When the supporting cast checks out against physical interior opponents or teams with size advantages, the offense stalls and the rebounding becomes a structural problem. Lewis is the reason to believe Villanova can get a first-round win. The depth is the reason to question anything beyond it. Utah State 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Jerrod Calhoun's Aggies went

This is helpful to determine the chances of teams being selected for the NCAA Tournament during March Madness. Some example use cases would be: to visualize how close a Q2 opponent is to becoming a higher quality Q1 opponent; to determine whether one team has more quality wins than another team; to check how many opportunities for quality wins remain on a team's schedule. This app is not associated with the NCAA or any institutions represented on this website. All marks and logos are trademarks of their respective owners. A green up arrow means that the team's NET rank is trending upwards compared to the previous rank, while a red down arrow means that the teams's NET rank is trending downwards. A gray neutral icon means that the ranking has not changed. The previous rank can be seen by opening the team's page. An opponent's current NET ranking is displayed in parenthesis next to the trending icon The quadrant system is used to identify which teams have more quality wins when it comes to Selection Sunday for the NCAA Tournament. Beating a Top-30 Team at home is considered equally as difficult as beating a Top-75 Team away: Quadrant 1: Home 1-30, Neutral 1-50, Away 1-75. Quadrant 2: Home 31-75, Neutral 51-100, Away 76-135. Quadrant 3: Home 76-160, Neutral 101-200, Away 135-240. Quadrant 4: Home 161-353, Neutral 201-353, Away 241-353. The Aggies have earned a 9 seed in the West Region. They will face Villanova in the Round of 64 on Friday in San Diego, as announced during Sunday’s bracket reveal show on CBS. This will be Utah State’s fourth consecutive NCAA Tournament appearance and 27th ticket to the Big Dance in program history. In Jerrod Calhoun’s first season with the program last year, the Aggies fell 72-47 to UCLA in a 7-10 round of 64 matchup. Since the school’s last Sweet 16 appearance in 1970, Utah State has a 2-20 record in NCAA Tournament contests, with the most recent such win coming in 2024 against 9-seeded TCU. Villanova ranks No. 33 in KenPom, having gone 24-8 in Kevin Willard’s first year piloting the Wildcats to finish third in the Big East. - **Villanova**: KenPom #33, Torvik #36, AdjEM +19.94, 24-8 record (Big East), strong defense (AdjDE 100.4, ranked 37th). **Critical injury**: Starting forward Matt Hodge suffered a season-ending ACL tear in late February, averaging 9.2 PPG/3.6 RPG — a meaningful frontcourt loss.

- **Betting market**: BettingPros noted Utah State is rated higher in the NET despite being the 9-seed. The market appears to lean Utah State.
- **Narrative**: One search result headline reads "Utah State Upsets Villanova in 8 vs. 9 March Madness Matchup" — Utah State's high-octane offense led by MJ Collins Jr. and Mason Falsev proved decisive.
- **Context**: Utah State won the Mountain West tournament (3rd title), entered on a high note, and carries a 28-6 record. Villanova's Hodge injury weakens their frontcourt depth heading into the tournament.
- **ESPN reaction snippet**: "Crank — Miami (Ohio), Utah State — for committee's decisions" suggests Utah State was underseeded at 9.

The combination of Utah State's superior NET ranking (#26 vs. Villanova's implied ~33-36), their Mountain West double-title, Villanova's significant frontcourt injury (Hodge ACL), Utah State's hot finish, and the betting market lean all point to Utah State.
